site.title: T-cell Cytokine Regulator Targets
site.subtitle: 128 context-specific cytokine regulators from genome-scale CD4+ T-cell Perturb-seq — druggability & therapeutic modality
site.footer: 128 context-specific regulators of 30 cytokines (Rest vs Stim8hr interaction, DESeq2 effect sizes). Scores are relative to this hit-gene cohort. See methods in the source repository.
site.attribution: Developed by Tanja Hann, with Claude Science.

citation.text: Zhu R., Dann E. et al. (2025) Genome-scale perturb-seq in primary human CD4+ T cells maps context-specific regulators of T cell programs and human immune traits. bioRxiv.

scores.label: Selectivity
scores.desc: Selectivity index (0.7·focus + 0.3·footprint), per gene. Higher = a more focused regulator of few cytokines rather than a broad master regulator. Relative to the 128-gene cohort.

scores.label: Protein druggability
scores.desc: Relative protein-level druggability: max(small-molecule pocket, antibody/biologic surface accessibility), renormalized across the 128-gene cohort (centered 0.5).

scores.label: RNA druggability
scores.desc: Relative RNA-level druggability: max(siRNA, ASO) targeting-site + stability/localization score, renormalized across the cohort (centered 0.5).

flags.label: Antibody route
flags.desc: Antibody/biologic is a viable protein sub-route (surface-accessible / localization-supported). Route-viability flag — can co-occur with an RNA nomination.

flags.label: Small-molecule route
flags.desc: Small-molecule is a viable protein sub-route (druggable pocket, relative protein score ≥ 0.3). Route-viability flag — can co-occur with an RNA nomination.

flags.label: ASO-nominated
flags.desc: Nominated therapeutic modality is an antisense oligonucleotide (RNA-level knockdown).

flags.label: siRNA-nominated
flags.desc: Nominated therapeutic modality is siRNA (RNA-level knockdown).
